title: Bisulfite pyrosequencing protocol for Human sperm DNA
steps: Extraction buffer of sperm DNA undetermined, 6mol/L guanidine thiocyanate undetermined, 5mol/L NaCl undetermined, 30% N-lauroylsarcosine sodium salt undetermined, 1mol/l dithiothreitol (DTT) undetermined, 10mg/ml proteinase K undetermined, Doubly deionized water
Add 150 μl of semen to 1.5 ml micro-centrifuge tube.
Wash with 1 ml of PBS (0.1 mol/L).
Centrifuge at 1500 ×g for 10 min at 4°C.
Repeat 2 times washing as step 3-4.
Add 0.5 ml extraction buffer into sperm pelle
Place in a 65°C water bath for 12 h.
Cool at room temperature.
Add 10 μl of RNase A (10 mg/ml), mix by pulse-vortexing for 15s, and incubate for 10 min at room temperature.
Briefly centrifuge the tube.
Add 510 μl of isopropanol and centrifuge at 10000 ×g for 10 min at 4°C.
Add 800 μl of ethanol (75%), and reverse mixing for dozens of times.
Incubate for 12 h at - 20°C.
Centrifuge at 10000 ×g for 10 min at 4°C. Then dry sample at room temperature.
Sperm DNA is dissolved in 50 μl of Elution Buffer.
Incubate in a 65°C water bath for 2 h.
Add 130 µl of the CT Conversion Reagent solution to 1000 ng of your DNA sample in a PCR tube.
Place the sample tube in a thermal cycler and perform the following steps*: ① 98°C for 10 minutes; ② 64°C for 2.5 hours; ③ 4°C.
Add 600 µl of M-Binding Buffer into a Zymo-Spin IC™ Column and place the column into a provided Collection Tube.
Load sample (from Step 2) into the Zymo-Spin IC™ Column containing the M-Binding Buffer. Close the cap and mix by inverting the column several times.
Centrifuge at full speed (>10,000 xg) for 30 seconds. Discard the flow-through.
Add 100 µl of M-Wash Buffer to the column. Spin at full speed for 30 seconds.
Add 200 µl of M-Desulphonation Buffer to the column and let stand at room temperature (20 °C – 30 °C) for 15-20 minutes. After the incubation, spin at full speed for 30 seconds.
Add 200 µl of M-Wash Buffer to the column. Spin at full speed for 30 seconds. Add another 200 µl of M-Wash Buffer and spin at top speed for 30 seconds.

Add 8 µl of M-Elution Buffer directly to the column matrix. Place the column into a 1.5 ml tube. Spin briefly at full speed to elute the DNA. Add 7 µl of M-Elution Buffer and additional repeated 1 time eluting was subsequently performed.
The DNA is ready for immediate analysis or can be stored at or below -20°C for later use.

PCR and pyrosequencing primers are designed and listed in Table 2. Reverse primer is conjugated to biotin. DMR Forward primer *Reverse primer Sequencing primerH19 GTATATGGGTATTTTTTGGAGGT ATATCCTATTCCCAAATAA TGGTTGTAGTTGTGGAATMEG3 GGGATTTTTGTTTTTTTTTGTAGTAGG CCAACCAAAACCCACCTATAAC TTTGGGGTTGGGGTTPEG3 TAATGAAAGTGTTTGAGATTTGTTG CCTATAAACAACCCCACACCTATAC GGGGGTAGTTGAGGTT Table 2 Primer sequences for PCR amplification DMR Forward primer *Reverse primer Sequencing primer H19 GTATATGGGTATTTTTTGGAGGT ATATCCTATTCCCAAATAA TGGTTGTAGTTGTGGAAT MEG3 GGGATTTTTGTTTTTTTTTGTAGTAGG CCAACCAAAACCCACCTATAAC TTTGGGGTTGGGGTT PEG3 TAATGAAAGTGTTTGAGATTTGTTG CCTATAAACAACCCCACACCTATAC GGGGGTAGTTGAGGTT Table 2 Primer sequences for PCR amplification
The PCR conditions are used as following: 94 °C for 15 min, followed by 45 cycles of 94 °C, 30 s, 56 °C, 30 s, 72°C, 30 s, and by a 72 °C final extension step for 10min.

All reactions are performed with provided PCR mixtures (total volume at 25μl) provided in Table 1. Each reaction also contain 2.5 ul of CoralLoad Concentrate (10x) for checking amplicons on an agarose gel. Components Volume (μl)Final concentrationPyroMark PCR Master Mix, 2×12.5 1×CoraLoad Concentrate, 10× 2.5 1×Q-solution, 5× 5 1×primer forward (10 uM) 0.5 0.2uMprimer reverse (10 uM) 0.5 0.2uMTemplate DNA 50ngFinal volume 25 Table 1 Components of PCR mixtures Components Volume (μl)Final concentration PyroMark PCR Master Mix, 2×12.5 1× CoraLoad Concentrate, 10× 2.5 1× Q-solution, 5× 5 1× primer forward (10 uM) 0.5 0.2uM primer reverse (10 uM) 0.5 0.2uM Template DNA 50ng Final volume 25 Table 1 Components of PCR mixtures
Add 40 μl of Binding Buffer, 3 μl of streptavidin-sepharose beads and 17 μl DDW into 20 μl of PCR products.
Seal film and shake at 1400 rpm for 10 min at room temperature.
PCR products on streptavidin-sepharose beads are washed with ethanol (10%) for 5s.
Place sample (from step 3) into denaturation solution for 5s.

Place sample (from step 4) into Wash Buffer for 10s for getting purified biotinylated single stranded PCR products. These single stranded PCR products are isolated using the Pyrosequencing Work Station.
Transfer purified biotinylated single stranded PCR products into PSQ 96 Plate Low with 40 μl annealing buffer and 1.6 μl sequencing primer (10μmol/L).
Heat PSQ 96 Plate Low at 80 ℃ for 2 minutes.
Undergo pyrosequencing on a Pyromark Q96 MD pyrosequencing instrument and sequence using PyroMark Gold Q96 kit.

The degree of methylation at each CpG site is determined using PyroMark CpG Software (Biotage AB, Uppsala, Sweden). Pyrosequencing assays are performed in duplicate in sequential runs (technical replicates), and the values show represent the mean methylation for each individual CpG site.
creator: Lu Zhao Xu
